timeoutSeconds.description: Number of seconds after which the probe times out.

periodSeconds.description: How often (in seconds) to perform the probe.

schema122.description:
The name of the ServiceAccount to use. If not set and create is
true, a name is generated using the fullname template

schema130.description: Comma-separated list of TLS cipher suites. If empty, uses Go defaults. Only applies to TLS 1.2.

schema133.description: Minimum TLS version (VersionTLS12 or VersionTLS13). Defaults to VersionTLS13.

schema134.description: Scrape interval. If not set, the Prometheus default scrape interval is used.

schema138.description: Minimum consecutive successes for the probe to be considered successful.

schema154.description: Number of failures before the probe is considered failed.

schema161.description: How often (in seconds) to perform the probe.

schema6.description:
frr contains configuration specific to the MetalLB FRR container,
for speaker running alongside FRR.
DEPRECATED: The FRR mode is deprecated and will be removed in a future
release. Use the frr-k8s mode (frrk8s.enabled) instead, which is now
the default BGP backend.

schema68.description:
relabel configs to apply to samples before ingestion.
- sourceLabels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_node_name]
separator: ;
regex: ^(.*)$
target_label: nodename
replacement: $1
action: replace

schema72.description:
metric relabel configs to apply to samples before ingestion.
- action: keep
regex: 'kube_(daemonset|deployment|pod|namespace|node|statefulset).+'
sourceLabels: [__name__]

successThreshold.description: Minimum consecutive successes for the probe to be considered successful.

failureThreshold.description: Number of failures before the probe is considered failed.

schema90.description: Comma-separated list of numeric CurveID values (e.g. 29,4588). See https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/tls#CurveID. If empty, uses Go defaults.

prometheus.description:
scrape annotations specifies whether to add Prometheus metric
auto-collection annotations to pods. See
https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/blob/release-2.1/documentation/examples/prometheus-kubernetes.yml
for a corresponding Prometheus configuration. Alternatively, you
may want to use the Prometheus Operator
(https://github.com/coreos/prometheus-operator) for more powerful
monitoring configuration. If you use the Prometheus operator, this
can be left at false.

namespace.description: The namespace where Prometheus is deployed. Required when ".Values.prometheus.rbacPrometheus == true" and "prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true".

controller.description:
controller contains configuration specific to the MetalLB cluster
controller.

logLevel.description: Controller log level. Must be one of: `all`, `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` or `none`

create.description: Specifies whether a ServiceAccount should be created

strategy.description:
ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#strategy
e.g:
strategy:
type: RollingUpdate
rollingUpdate:

frr-k8s.description:
Values passed to the frr-k8s subchart (note the hyphen in "frr-k8s").

https://github.com/metallb/frr-k8s/blob/main/charts/frr-k8s/values.yaml

validationFailurePolicy.description: Validation failure policy for CRDs. Can be Fail or Ignore.

frrk8s.description:
controller contains configuration specific to the FRRK8s controller
daemonset.

affinity.description:
Affinity for pod assignment.
Selects which runtime class will be used by the pod.

alwaysBlock.description: A comma separated list of cidrs to always block for incoming routes.

bgpDebounceTimeout.description: (integer) BGP debounce timeout for FRR configuration reloads, in milliseconds. Default (when unset) is 3000 ms.This feature is experimental

disableCertRotation.description:
Specifies whether the cert rotator works as part of the webhook.
Note: Enabling this proved useful for the webhook's stability when it is redeployed multiple times in succession.

frr.description: frr contains configuration specific to the FRR container,

acceptIncomingBGPConnections.description: Allow FRR to accept incoming BGP connections.

resources.description: Resource limits and requests for the FRR container.
Resource limits and requests for the FRR metrics container.

pollInterval.description: Polling interval for FRR status updates.

resources.description: Resource limits and requests for the FRR status container.

tag.description: The frr-k8s image tag. If not set, defaults to the chart appVersion.

labels.description: Additional labels to add to the pod.

initialDelaySeconds.description: Number of seconds after the container has started before liveness probes are initiated.

timeoutSeconds.description: Number of seconds after which the probe times out.

logLevel.description: Controller log level that is passed as a CLI flag. Must be one of: `all`, `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` or `none`

priorityClassName.description: Priority class name for the pod.

initialDelaySeconds.description: Number of seconds after the container has started before readiness probes are initiated.

timeoutSeconds.description: Number of seconds after which the probe times out.

resources.description: Resource limits and requests for the reloader container.

Resource limits and requests for the frr-k8s controller container.
limits:
cpu: 100m
memory: 100Mi

restartOnRotatorSecretRefresh.description: Specifies whether the pod restarts when the rotator refreshes the cert secret. Useful for webhook stability during redeployments.

runtimeClassName.description: Runtime class name for the pod.

annotations.description:
Additional annotations to add to the ServiceAccount.
Defines a secret name for the controller to generate a memberlist encryption secret
By default secretName: {{ "metallb.fullname" }}-memberlist
secretName:

create.description: Specifies whether a ServiceAccount should be created.

name.description: The name of the ServiceAccount to use. If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template.

tolerateMaster.description: Tolerate master nodes for pod scheduling.

updateStrategy.description:
ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/manage-daemon/update-daemon-set/
StrategyType
Can be set to RollingUpdate or OnDelete

fullnameOverride.description: String to override the default fully qualified app name.

nameOverride.description: String to override the default chart name.

prometheus.description:
scrape annotations specifies whether to add Prometheus metric
auto-collection annotations to pods. See
https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/blob/release-2.1/documentation/examples/prometheus-kubernetes.yml
for a corresponding Prometheus configuration. Alternatively, you
may want to use the Prometheus Operator
(https://github.com/coreos/prometheus-operator) for more powerful
monitoring configuration. If you use the Prometheus operator, this
can be left at false.

namespace.description: The namespace where Prometheus is deployed. Required when ".Values.prometheus.rbacPrometheus == true" and "prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true".

rbacPrometheus.description: Give Prometheus permission to scrape metallb's namespace.

scrapeAnnotations.description: Add Prometheus metric auto-collection annotations to pods.

secureMetricsPort.description: Port frr-k8s will listen on for secure metrics.

serviceAccount.description: The service account used by Prometheus. Required when ".Values.prometheus.rbacPrometheus == true" and "prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true"

additionalLabels.description: Additional labels to add to the ServiceMonitor.

annotations.description: Optional additional annotations for the controller serviceMonitor.

enabled.description:
Enable Prometheus ServiceMonitor for frr-k8s metrics.
The FRR-K8s BGP/BFD metrics are exposed with the "frrk8s_" prefix
(e.g. frrk8s_bgp_session_up, frrk8s_bfd_session_up).
To rename them to the legacy "metallb_" prefix for backward compatibility
with existing dashboards or alerts, enable and configure metric relabelings:
metricRelabelings:
- sourceLabels: [__name__]
regex: "frrk8s_bgp_(.*)"
targetLabel: "__name__"
replacement: "metallb_bgp_$1"
- sourceLabels: [__name__]
regex: "frrk8s_bfd_(.*)"
targetLabel: "__name__"
replacement: "metallb_bfd_$1"

metricRelabelings.description:
Metric relabel configs to apply to samples before ingestion.
- action: keep
regex: 'kube_(daemonset|deployment|pod|namespace|node|statefulset).+'
sourceLabels: [__name__]

relabelings.description:
Relabel configs to apply to samples before ingestion.
- sourceLabels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_node_name]
separator: ;
regex: ^(.*)$
target_label: nodename
replacement: $1
action: replace

tlsConfig.description: Optional TLS configuration for the controller serviceMonitor, in case secure metrics are enabled.

create.description: Specifies whether to install and use RBAC rules.

metricsTLSSecret.description: The name of the secret to be mounted in the pods to provide TLS certificates for metrics endpoints. If not present, a self-signed certificate is auto-generated.

frrk8s.description:
frrk8s contains the configuration related to using an frrk8s instance
(github.com/metallb/frr-k8s) as the backend for the BGP implementation.
This allows configuring additional frr parameters in combination to those
applied by MetalLB.

enabled.description: If set, enables frrk8s as a backend. This is mutually exclusive to frr mode.

external.description: If true, uses an external frr-k8s installation instead of the bundled subchart.

namespace.description: Namespace where external frr-k8s is installed (only used when external=true).

apiPort.description: to override internal k8s api targetPort
to override internal k8s api targetPort

defaultDeny.description: if set, a default deny network policy will be installed in the metallb namespace

enabled.description: if set, networkpolicies for metallb components will be installed in the metallb namespace

metricsPort.description:
port both controller and speaker will listen on for metrics (always HTTPS).
Matches the port kube-rbac-proxy previously served on.

namespace.description:
the namespace where prometheus is deployed
required when " .Values.prometheus.rbacPrometheus == true " and " .Values.prometheus.podMonitor.enabled=true or prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true "

serviceAccount.description:
the service account used by prometheus
required when " .Values.prometheus.rbacPrometheus == true " and " .Values.prometheus.podMonitor.enabled=true or prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true "

additionalLabels.description: optional additional labels for the controller serviceMonitor

serviceMonitor.description:
Prometheus Operator ServiceMonitors. To be used as an alternative
to podMonitor, supports secure metrics.

annotations.description: optional additional annotations for the controller serviceMonitor

serviceMonitor.description:
Prometheus Operator ServiceMonitors. To be used as an alternative
to podMonitor, supports secure metrics.

metricsPort.description:
port both controller and speaker will listen on for metrics (always HTTPS).
Matches the port kube-rbac-proxy previously served on.

namespace.description:
the namespace where prometheus is deployed
required when " .Values.prometheus.rbacPrometheus == true " and " .Values.prometheus.podMonitor.enabled=true or prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true "

excludePools.description: Exclude the pools matching the regular expression from triggering the alert.
Exclude the pools matching the regular expression from triggering the alert.

rbacPrometheus.description: prometheus doesn't have the permission to scrape all namespaces so we give it permission to scrape metallb's one

scrapeAnnotations.description:
scrape annotations specifies whether to add Prometheus metric
auto-collection annotations to pods. See
https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/blob/release-2.1/documentation/examples/prometheus-kubernetes.yml
for a corresponding Prometheus configuration. Alternatively, you
may want to use the Prometheus Operator
(https://github.com/coreos/prometheus-operator) for more powerful
monitoring configuration. If you use the Prometheus operator, this
can be left at false.

serviceAccount.description:
the service account used by prometheus
required when " .Values.prometheus.rbacPrometheus == true " and " .Values.prometheus.podMonitor.enabled=true or prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true "

additionalLabels.description: optional additional labels for the controller serviceMonitor

annotations.description: optional additional annotations for the controller serviceMonitor

tlsConfig.description:
optional tls configuration for the controller serviceMonitor, in case
secure metrics are enabled.

additionalLabels.description: optional additional labels for the speaker serviceMonitor

annotations.description: optional additional annotations for the speaker serviceMonitor

tlsConfig.description:
optional tls configuration for the speaker serviceMonitor, in case
secure metrics are enabled.

serviceMonitor.description:
Prometheus Operator ServiceMonitors. To be used as an alternative
to podMonitor, supports secure metrics.

create.description: create specifies whether to install and use RBAC rules.

runtimeClassName.description: Selects which runtime class will be used by the pod.

name.description:
The name of the ServiceAccount to use. If not set and create is
true, a name is generated using the fullname template

speaker.description:
speaker contains configuration specific to the MetalLB speaker
daemonset.

bgpDebounceTimeout.description: BGP debounce timeout for FRR configuration reloads, in milliseconds. Only applies when BGP type is frr. Default (when unset) is 3000 ms. This feature is experimental

frr.description:
frr contains configuration specific to the MetalLB FRR container,
for speaker running alongside FRR.
DEPRECATED: The FRR mode is deprecated and will be removed in a future
release. Use the frr-k8s mode (frrk8s.enabled) instead, which is now
the default BGP backend.

logLevel.description: Speaker log level. Must be one of: `all`, `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` or `none`

enabled.description: When enabled: false, the speaker pods must run on all nodes

resources.description:
Defines a secret name for the controller to generate a memberlist encryption secret
By default secretName: {{ "metallb.fullname" }}-memberlist
secretName:

runtimeClassName.description: Selects which runtime class will be used by the pod.

create.description: Specifies whether a ServiceAccount should be created

controllerMetricsTLSSecret.description: The name of the secret to be mounted in the controller pod to provide TLS certificates for metrics endpoints. If not present, a self-signed certificate is auto-generated.

speakerMetricsTLSSecret.description: The name of the secret to be mounted in the speaker pod to provide TLS certificates for metrics endpoints. If not present, a self-signed certificate is auto-generated.
